Curt Clifton's microblog

This morning’s macchiato was so pretty the barista stopped to photograph it. https://t.co/hWzMdBoDQ4

26 Feb 2017

Fun but icy day of skiing at Steven’s. I wiped out under a lift and earned a well deserved Nelson laugh from above. https://t.co/EKlbBSnn5M

25 Feb 2017

As if the Pumpkinführer even knows that “military” can mean precision. Hell, this dipshit doesn’t even know what “precision” means. https://t.co/FAzVdcORoe

24 Feb 2017

One of those nights where I feel lucky to live in Seattle doing what I do with a community of people that I love. ❤

23 Feb 2017

I just love fixing crashers when there’s a solid repro.

23 Feb 2017

New wheels. https://t.co/Qa2BVXB4Fo

20 Feb 2017

Test rode a couple of e-bikes today. Woah, that’s fun!

19 Feb 2017

Font nerd and designer friends: I’m thinking of using Livory for body text in an app. Any recommendations for a display font to go with it?

19 Feb 2017

Returning to reading twitter after a month off. I’m going to try to tune the stress out of my feed. We’ll see how long this last.

18 Feb 2017

Finally saw Hidden Figures tonight. Beautiful, frustrating, inspiring. We’ve come so far and have so far to go. #fb

17 Feb 2017

Next three stages: (T) drawknife, (M) spokeshave and chisel, (B) card scraper https://t.co/dDqbCbDfhF

13 Feb 2017

I’m working on my own container view controller and want children to have a proper bottomLayoutGuide. Supported, or do I have to swizzle?

12 Feb 2017

Drove a Car2go Mercedes for the first time today. Nice!

12 Feb 2017

Mischief managed. Time to head home. 🏡 🇺🇸

10 Feb 2017

More than sixteen inches of snow with another 7–10 expected. The village is beautiful. https://t.co/5pVsnzioc8

09 Feb 2017

Thirteen inches of powder overnight and it’s still snowing! https://t.co/36nwvsqLvU

09 Feb 2017

Forecast is for 40-55 cm of snow in the next 24 hours. Hotel has one room left for Thursday night. Stay extended. 😀⛷🇨🇦

At 1.9 miles between support towers, there’s lots of time to pose for photos. https://t.co/sypDvrzK2L
